mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings higher and supports positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive over a trailing 2-quarter period but forward 60-trading-day SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 5.0% and forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in at least 60% of such observations over a rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 3.0% and retail sales are declining (below $320 billion), the combined signal of monetary tightening and weakening consumption indicates an economic slowdown that depresses equity prices over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If both trigger conditions hold but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions, rising housing starts signal robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as cyclical optimism builds.
failure_condition: If HOUST is rising in an early-cycle phase but forward 5-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of such observations, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects increasing manufacturing output and economic activity, which generally leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month for three consecutive months and forward 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 30% of such cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year rise in CPI inflation above 3.0% signals overheating and impending monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0% and forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of such episodes over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment exceeds 133 million and retail sales surpass $340 billion, the joint signal of strong labor demand and robust consumer spending indicates healthy economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If both conditions are met but forward 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 35% of such observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During recoveries from recession, rising payroll employment signals improving labor market conditions and increasing consumer income, which supports equity prices positively over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS is rising (month-over-month growth for three consecutive months) during a recovery period but forward 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 40% of such cases, the hypothesis is falsified.
